name: Daniel Osei
notableTransitions: Promoted to Senior Analyst with team-lead scope while remaining without formal management title
Took ownership of a three-person workstream and an executive revenue dashboard used in board meetings
Redesigned quarterly forecasting model, reducing forecast variance from 18% to 7%
Mentored two junior analysts on SQL and dashboarding standards, expanding influence beyond pure IC work
tensions: Title-vs-impact gap: leadership responsibilities without formal people-management title
Overlap/ambiguity in role labeling (Analyst vs Senior Analyst within overlapping timeframes)
Possible plateau: long tenure at the same title despite expanding scope and impact
Limited external credentials or formal progression signals (no documented promotion to manager)
